commit_message:
Fix SAML test initialisation flakiness (#139149)

Remove a short window of time during which the metadata was available and potentially could be cached by a background thread, before the actual test method is called. The test method assumes that the metada is not cached, hence that could have created the flakiness. Also, change the way the metadataEnabled field is initialised: from putIfAbsent() to put(). That latter was causing the test to fail, depending on the order in which tests were executed.

Closes #139067

commit_message:
allocation: fix up random long usage with inclusive bound (#139163)

In the write load constraint monitor tests, the criteria for specifying a
non-hotspot node generated a random queue latency between 0 and the hotspot
threshold setting. In usage, the random number generation used the threshold as
an inclusive bound, while it needed to be an exclusive bound. This became an
issue recently, with the addition of tests that specify a certain number of
hotspot nodes, and remove them individually throughout the test
(testHotspotCountTurnsOff).

Fixes: #139161

commit_message:
Force merge sparse_vector test index to stabilize pruning (#146447)

The averageTokenFreqRatio used by token pruning depends on the
per-segment max unique token count. With multiple segments the
max can be lower than the total, inflating the average and
flipping borderline pruning decisions. Force merge to one segment
after the bulk index so term statistics are deterministic.

Closes #139196

issue_title: TransportGetTransformStatsAction can eat a transport thread, causing cluster instability
issue_body:
### Elasticsearch Version

_any_

### Installed Plugins

_No response_

### Java Version

_bundled_

### OS Version

_any_

### Problem Description

Transform stats does a fair bit of work just sitting in `direct` executor. This is not good. Depending on the scale of the cluster, if multiple users call the stats call for transforms, it could disconnect node from a cluster.

We should:

1. Make this way way cheaper, only consider transform tasks (do filtering directly in tasks request, not after gathering all tasks on the node) and be careful about ginormous strings being created and concatenated
2. Consider moving this off of direct executor.

Some additional notes:

> This is 100% a bug that needs reporting to the transform (ML?) folks. We seem to be looping over all tasks, filtering them by description (not even filtering by action first??). Building the description of a single search task might not be too costly but isn't exactly free either, and building a description of them all could be very expensive, and even worse I'm pretty sure no search tasks could possibly have a matching description anyway. Moreover this is all happening on the transport_worker for no good reason and needs to be moved elsewhere as a matter of some urgency.

### Steps to Reproduce

Many nodes, many transforms == no fun for the cluster.

commit_message:
[Transform] Reduce task match load (#139857)

* [Transform] Reduce task match load

Move `TransportGetTransformStatsAction` to the generic threadpool, and
match `Task` based on java type first before comparing the description to
prevent iterating over a large abount of transform ids for tasks that
are not `TransformTask`.

Resolve #139252

* Update docs/changelog/139857.yaml

* check for null; clean up

* switch to management

* refactor

---------

Co-authored-by: Jan Kuipers <jan.kuipers@elastic.co>

issue_title: ESQL: FoldNull can wrongly fold COALESCE to null
issue_body:
Reproducer:
```
ROW x = null
| EVAL z = COALESCE(x, "1")
| EVAL append_coalesce = MV_APPEND("2", COALESCE(x, "1"))
| EVAL append_z = MV_APPEND("2", z)

       x       |       z       |append_coalesce|   append_z    
---------------+---------------+---------------+---------------
null           |1              |null           |[2, 1]   
```
`append_coalesce` should be the same as `append_z`.

Looking at the change logger output, it looks like the problem is inside `FoldNull`:

```
[2025-12-11T10:09:59,716][TRACE][o.e.x.e.o.L.changes      ] [runTask-0] Rule logical.FoldNull applied with change
Limit[1000[INTEGER],false,false]                                                                                      = Limit[1000[INTEGER],false,false]
\_Eval[[COALESCE(x{r}#14,1[KEYWORD]) AS z#17, MVAPPEND(2[KEYWORD],COALESCE(x{r}#14,1[KEYWORD])) AS append_coalesce#20 ! \_Eval[[COALESCE(x{r}#14,1[KEYWORD]) AS z#17, null[KEYWORD] AS append_coalesce#20, MVAPPEND(2[KEYWORD],z{r}#17) AS ap
, MVAPPEND(2[KEYWORD],z{r}#17) AS append_z#23]]                                                                       ! pend_z#23]]
  \_Row[[null[NULL] AS x#14]]                                                                                         =   \_Row[[null[NULL] AS x#14]]
```

We shouldn't have folded to null because `COALESCE(null, "1")` should be `"1"`.

issue_title: ESQL: Prunning inline stats issue when dropping groupings
issue_body:
### Description

```
FROM employees
| KEEP languages, salary
| INLINE STATS languages1 = MV_AVG(languages), avg = AVG(salary) BY languages
| INLINE STATS languages2 = SUM(languages1), avg = AVG(salary) BY languages
| DROP languages
| SORT salary
| LIMIT 10
```

Results in 

```
Plan [EsqlProject[[salary{f}#113, languages1{r}#95, languages2{r}#102, avg{r}#105]]] optimized incorrectly due to missing references [languages1{r}#95]
```

Very similar with https://github.com/elastic/elasticsearch/issues/138283, but not quite the same thing.
commit_message:
ESQL: remove incorrect inline stats pruning (#141056)

Removes incorrect pruning in `inline stats` that lacked taking into
consideration the fact that groupings can be multi-valued. Fixes
https://github.com/elastic/elasticsearch/issues/139359#issuecomment-3773241566
(grouping by an mv fields can lead to inconsistencies in certain
queries) Fixes https://github.com/elastic/elasticsearch/issues/139359
Fixes https://github.com/elastic/elasticsearch/issues/140757

This also extracts a bunch of tests methods from the huge
`LogicalPlanOptimizerTests` and moves them to a new test class -
`PruneColumnsTests` which should include most of the tests that have in
the spotlight the `PruneColumns` optimization rule.

commit_message:
Fix native array integration tests (#139577)

In #139093 we updated the NativeArrayIntegrationTestCase to extract the 
single value out of arrays of length 1. This prevents the creation of the
.offsets field (as it's not need for single-valued fields), which then
fails the later assertion in the test which checks for the presence of
this .offsets field.

This PR fixes the test failures by reverting the logic to collapse the
arrays, instead maintaining single-valued arrays as-is.

commit_message:
Sort the values of a legacy histogram during downsampling (#140771)

When indexing legacy histograms in elasticsearch we require that their
values are sorted. 

When downsampling is using the aggregate method, it merges the
histograms to a single one and indexes it in the target index. We
noticed that some merging algorithms do not guarantee that the centroids
will be sorted. 

This PR adds code that sorts the centroids before they are indexed to
the target index to avoid indexing failures.

Fixes #139382

commit_message:
fix and unmute SamlRedirectTests (#139459)

SamlRedirectTests are comparing request values, which are compressed
with zlib deflate. This function is not guaranteed to always be
deterministic and can change when zlib version is updated. That is
exactly what happened. For that reason, instead of relying on hardcoded
result of compression, the test is not comparing uncompressed values.

Resolves https://github.com/elastic/elasticsearch/issues/139389 Resolves
https://github.com/elastic/elasticsearch/issues/139390 Resolves
https://github.com/elastic/elasticsearch/issues/139391 Resolves
https://github.com/elastic/elasticsearch/issues/139392

commit_message:
fix and unmute SamlRedirectTests (#139459)

SamlRedirectTests are comparing request values, which are compressed
with zlib deflate. This function is not guaranteed to always be
deterministic and can change when zlib version is updated. That is
exactly what happened. For that reason, instead of relying on hardcoded
result of compression, the test is not comparing uncompressed values.

Resolves https://github.com/elastic/elasticsearch/issues/139389 Resolves
https://github.com/elastic/elasticsearch/issues/139390 Resolves
https://github.com/elastic/elasticsearch/issues/139391 Resolves
https://github.com/elastic/elasticsearch/issues/139392

commit_message:
ESQL: Prevent circular alias references in `DeduplicateAggs` (#139175)

## Summary

Fixes circular reference detection in alias chains during
`DeduplicateAggs` optimizations.

## Problem

The `PushDownEnrich` optimization can create circular references when
pushing `Enrich` past `Project` operations, causing
`ql_illegal_argument_exception: Potential cycle detected` errors when
`DeduplicateAggs`.

Example query:

```esql
FROM languages_lookup 
| RENAME language_name AS message  
| ENRICH languages_policy ON language_code 
| STATS a = count(to_lower(language_name)), b = count(language_name)
```

## Changes

Skip aliases that would create circular references by checking if
`alias.toAttribute()` equals the resolved `alias.child()`.

Closes #138346 Closes #139541
